channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: ADHD Paralysis: Why You Freeze When There's Too Much to Do (And How to Break Out of It)
item.description: You have twelve things to do. You know what they are. You know they matter. And yet you are sitting on the couch, staring at your phone, feeling the guilt pile up like a physical weight. You are...

item.title: What Breaks MCP Servers in Production (MCP Best Practicies)?
item.description: Our team runs several MCP servers in production. Two of them are remote multi-user servers. The first is an analytics gateway: a semantic layer for company data, where metrics have one...

item.title: Replacing ntpdate with chronyc on Anolis OS for GBase 8a Cluster Time Sync
item.description: When a gbase database cluster deployed on Anolis OS 8.9 is powered off for an extended period, the system clock can drift significantly. Since modern Anolis releases no longer ship ntpdate and...

item.title: The 5 Types of AI Agent Memory Every TypeScript Developer Should Know
item.description: Most developers try to fix AI agents with better prompts. In practice, most agent problems are memory problems. The agent forgets context. It repeats the same mistake. It uses outdated...

item.title: The Engineer Who Waits and the Engineer Who Hunts
item.description: I manage two teams. They are structured almost identically — same size, same roles, same tools, same access to leadership, same business domain. They receive the same inputs and operate under the...

item.title: Mail Tracker
item.description: MailTracker will hook into all outgoing emails from Laravel and inject a tracking code into it. It will also store the rendered email in the database. There is also an interface to view sent...

item.title: A single-binary Rust worker that drains WordPress job queues
item.description: WordPress is a fine place to decide that work needs to happen and a terrible place to do heavy work. Everything you run inside a request competes with your visitors; everything you run in WP-Cron...

item.title: From the Fantasy of Local Models to a Truly Controllable Sports Agent
item.description: Looking back, this project did not begin as a fully designed AI agent project. In fact, I had not even decided from the outset that I wanted to build a sports question-answering system. The...

item.title: Bringing GA4 into an MCP server: what it actually takes to make analytics agent-friendly
item.description: I build GSC Wizard, an SEO analytics tool. A while back I added an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so people could ask an AI assistant — ChatGPT, Claude, whatever — questions about their own...

item.title: AI Without a Backend: The Browser's Built-in AI APIs for Web Developers
item.description: For years, "adding AI to your web app" meant the same thing: sign up for an API key, wire up a backend proxy so you don't leak that key to the browser, meter your token usage, handle rate limits,...

item.title: US rare earths flow to Asia as domestic demand is slow to emerge
item.description: Miners backed by Trump admin. sell to Japan, South Korea despite push to develop domestic supply chain.

item.title: Fi’s Starlink-enabled pet tracker found my dog when LTE couldn’t
item.description: Fi Ultra is the first Starlink-enabled pet tracker you can buy. It expands on GPS and LTE trackers, adding automatic failover to T-Mobile's T-Satellite-branded direct-to-cell service when...

item.title: New Ghost Phishing Wave Is Breaking Traditional Email Security
item.description: A recent EvilTokens campaign targeting businesses across the US and Europe is exposing a new email security blind spot. This “ghost phishing” technique keeps the malicious page hidden until it...

item.title: SCMBANKER Malware Uses ClickFix Lures to Target Mexican Banking Users
item.description: A new banking fraudulent operation is targeting customers of Mexican banks, fintech, payment processors, and cryptocurrency exchanges using ClickFix lures. The activity cluster, tracked by Elastic...

item.title: Felons, Fraudsters Flog Offensive Cybersecurity Startup
item.description: A cybersecurity startup dangling millions of dollars to acquire zero-day security vulnerabilities in popular software is run by a pair of far-right conspiracy theorists and convicted felons whose...

item.title: The Download: worms fight pollution, and geoengineering faces reality
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure...

item.title: Former OpenAI exec Kevin Weil is now on the board of Stoke Space
item.description: Kevin Weil's new role at Stoke Space suggests reusable rockets are the next hot thing in Silicon Valley.

item.title: The Steam Machine fits my TV, my desk, and my life
item.description: For the last couple weeks, I've been in an extremely lucky position: I've been spending a lot of time playing games on Valve's Steam Machine. We gave the Steam Machine a 6, and I don't disagree...

item.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures
item.description: New research shows that a signed Git commit's hash is not the one-of-a-kind name that much of the software world assumes it to be. Given any signed commit, someone without the signing key can mint...

item.title: The Verification Step Is the New ATO Battleground in 2026
item.description: For years, account takeover (ATO) followed a predictable script. Attackers bought stolen credentials in bulk, ran them through automated tools, and waited for matches. Credential stuffing was...

item.title: GitHub Copilot Refuses Harmful Requests in Chat, Then Writes Them in Code
item.description: An AI coding assistant that refuses to answer a dangerous request in its chat box can answer it anyway if the same request is broken into small, ordinary-looking steps inside a code editor. That...

item.title: Cybersecurity and the Gap Between Skill and Ability
item.description: Last week, national security agencies from the Five Eyes—that’s the rich, English-language-speaking countries club—jointly released a statement warning of the increasing cyber risks of AI models:...

item.title: Pickup Artist Mystery Has an AI Girlfriend
item.description: A new book claims that Mystery, who teaches awkward men how to hit on women, had sex and smoked weed with an AI chatbot named Miss Shira Always.

item.title: The robotaxi law that could ban Tesla
item.description: For more than a decade, one question has loomed over the race to build autonomous vehicles: Are cameras alone enough to safely replace human drivers, or do truly driverless cars need additional,...

item.title: This Former DeepMind Exec Thinks the AI Arms Race Could End in Disaster
item.description: Verity Harding tells WIRED that the US government’s nationalistic attitude toward AI is evidence that a worst-case scenario is taking shape.

item.title: China-Linked UAT-7810 Expands ORB Network With New LONGLEASH Malware
item.description: A Chinese threat actor tracked as UAT-7810 is actively refining its bespoke malware to expand its Operational Relay Box (ORB) network by breaking into internet-facing networking devices. According...

item.title: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
item.description: Samsung has announced that its next Galaxy Unpacked launch event will be held on July 22nd, with the tagline: "A new shape unfolds." It's long been rumored that Samsung is about to expand its...

item.title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
item.description: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.

item.title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
item.description: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11B valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.

item.title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
item.description: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
